condition_records.text: Polarity/wrapper: stated as answer(False) ↔ P where P = (∀ A density-zero, ∃ B, A ⊆ B+B ∧ count = o(√N)) — FC house style for a disproved yes/no question (cf. FC 263.parts.ii). jayyhk's hosted erdos_333 is the direct negation ¬P; plby has NO erdos-named endpoint at all — its final main_obstruction is the conjunction (specific counterexample A has density zero) ∧ ¬∃ B …, which i

The floor is load-bearing for fidelity: with

condition_records.text: 'What is the largest integer not of the form ...' rendered as `IsGreatest {m : Z | not exists c, m = sum ...} target`; both hosted theorems state `target n not-in Representable n && forall x : Z, x > target n -> x in Representable n`. Logically equivalent: membership plus the contrapositive of the upper-bound half of IsGreatest. IsGreatest is the idiomatic Mathlib packaging.; R

condition_records.text: Count vocabulary: draft uses Mathlib's SimpleGraph.copyCount G (cycleGraph 5) (number of subgraphs of G isomorphic to C5); both hosted proofs define numC5 G = #{labelled C5 maps Fin 5 -> V} / 10 (labelled embeddings divided by |D5| = 10). The two counts are equal on every graph (each C5-subgraph carries exactly 10 labellings), so this is neither a strengthening nor a weakening.; Form: draft wraps

FC's erdos_258 quantifies over all a_n → ∞ and asserts the divisor series is irrational, matching the boxed problem.
